Greek life exists (25%) but not required; social scene still cliquey
Kelly's bar is the only reliable nightlife; others stay empty

Limited data — not a major topic of student discussion

key_points: One freshman described failed suicide attempt and panic attacks from workload
Workload hits hard immediately — 'classes and workload started right off the bat'
No strong praise for counseling resources; support infrastructure unclear
key_quotes: I've been in and out of the psyche ward. I've been so overwhelmed by everything, especially the classes and workload that started right off the bat
I don't have any close friends... my relationship isn't going the best right now

key_points: Financial aid office slow and error-prone — multiple students ghosted or delayed
Full sticker price runs ~$75k/year; little need-based aid for middle class
Merit scholarships exist (Presidential, full tuition) but highly competitive
key_quotes: Villanova would cost $75k a year. This is way out of my price range
They do not respond to my emails even though there's a day left to figure this shit out
I got accepted ED1, but they give no aid whatsoever. They allowed me to withdraw from the bind because of it

key_points: VSB (business school) is the crown jewel; CLAS feels like an afterthought
Mandatory theology courses surprise non-Catholic students; 2+ required
Blackboard LMS widely disliked — 'literal garbage' interface, unlike Canvas
